title: Lobby.ts network-error catches discard the exception with zero logging
evidence: Six catch blocks in the create/join/browse/ready-up/update-player/leave-room flows all follow `catch (err) { this.onlineError = 'Network error. Try again.'; ...; this.render(); }` (e.g. line 898-902, create_room; 1048-1052, join_room; 1118-1122, list_rooms poll; 1586-1590, ready_up; 1752-1756, update_player) — the caught `err` variable is bound but never read: no console.error/warn, no context. leave_room's catch (1780-1782) doesn't even bind a used variable, just a comment 'Best-effort — leave the room locally regardless.' This is a different code path than NetworkClient.ts's submitAction/resyncLog catches, which DO log (e.g. NetworkClient.ts:802 `console.error('NetworkClient: submit_action network error:', err)`), and different from the #67/#79 desync signals.
impact: There is no error-tracking service in this stack (tech-stack.md: no APM/Sentry) — the browser console is the only diagnostic surface. When a player reports 'create room / join / ready up doesn't work', the actual failure (CORS rejection, JSON parse error, DNS failure, 5xx body shape mismatch) is silently discarded; only a generic 'Network error. Try again.' UI toast survives, indistinguishable across all failure causes and unrecoverable after the fact.
recommendation: Log `err` with the operation name and, where in scope, code/roomId/playerId at each of the 6 catch sites (mirroring the pattern already used in NetworkClient.ts's submitAction/resyncLog catches) before setting the UI error string.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 6 Lobby.ts network-error catch blocks logs the caught error (console.error/warn) with an operation label before/alongside setting onlineError.
